reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome real download+run on WASM, standalone inference cross-check, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser run (headless Chrome, WASM, fresh profile → 'Download model (~470 MB)' → click → auto-run): the model reranked the mixed EN/JA/ES/DE houseplant-travel default set and reordered it — #1 (was #2) Japanese passage '旅行前に自動給水スパイクを使えば…二週間は湿った' logit 1.66, promoted above the English/Spanish/German candidates for an ENGLISH query; #2 (was #5) Spanish 'Agrupa las macetas lejos del sol' logit -1.02; 6 rows, 309 ms, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. Standalone browser cross-check (cross-encoder/mmarco-mMiniLMv2-L12-H384-v1, fp32, WASM): logits shape [5,1]; ordering EN-relevant 10.39 > ES-relevant 10.11 > ZH-offtopic(Tokyo) 1.51 > DE-loose -3.18 > unrelated-recipe -8.13.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified real WASM run on the actual page; cross-lingual reranking confirmed (JA passage #1 for an EN query).

rubric.evidence: Cross-encoder output is one relevance logit per (query,passage) pair (num_labels=1 → logits [N,1]); higher = more relevant. Confirmed the tensor is a REAL scoring head (logits [5,1]), not last_hidden_state. Semantics correct and cross-lingual: a Japanese passage ranked #1 for an English query, unrelated recipe ranked last.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (cross-encoder/mmarco-mMiniLMv2-L12-H384-v1, linked), task (text-classification used as a cross-encoder reranker, num_labels=1), architecture (XLM-RoBERTa MiniLMv2, 12-layer distilled from XLM-R Large), backend (WebAssembly), and the honest quantisation note (fp32 model.onnx; the repo's int8 files use hardware-specific names transformers.js can't auto-resolve, so fp32 is the portable path). Inference runs in a module Web Worker (models/mmarco-reranker/worker.js). Unlike jina-reranker-v2 the config already carries model_type:'xlm-roberta', so a plain load routes correctly — documented on the page.
rubric.notes: dtype honesty: fp32 disclosed with the concrete reason quantised files aren't auto-resolvable.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). Headless fresh profile showed 'Download model (~470 MB)'; after the click it auto-loaded to state=ready and auto-ran the default rerank. Cached auto-init is provided by the same shared loader used across all built demos (per-model 'Clear cached model' + storage readout).
rubric.notes: Fresh-profile Download + auto-init-to-ready verified on the real page.

rubric.evidence: A role=status aria-live #runStatus region reports 'Scoring query–passage pairs…' / 'Done.' / errors; the shared loader streams download progress and offers Retry on failure; the WASM path needs no GPU so it works offline once cached. Failures surface on the page ('Reranking failed: …'), not only the console.
rubric.notes: Runs on WASM — no WebGPU required; degrades honestly.

rubric.evidence: Query input (Enter reranks), passages textarea, and the Rerank button are real, labelled, keyboard-operable; the practical page adds language-preset chips (ask the same question in EN/ES/JA/DE), the wild page adds six language chips over a fixed answer bank, and the multi-model page adds a shortlist top-k number input. The results list, before/after view and cross-encoder-vs-lexical contrast render from the real run.

The ranking, logits, sigmoid probabilities and before/after view are all produced by the on-device cross-encoder run (verified real output above). The lexical-overlap column is clearly labelled as a naive contrast baseline, never presented as the model's ranking. No canned result anywhere.

Buttons expose accessible names; the query input and passages textarea have associated labels; state announced via role=status aria-live. No content images without alt (text/table only). The at-a-glance table uses a scoped overflow-wrap:anywhere so long ids wrap instead of forcing overflow.

Browser matrix (scripts/responsive-check.mjs): desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both measured 0px horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, clean console + network. Screenshots reviewed: desktop (dark) + mobile (light) of the main page — the at-a-glance table wraps the long model id, the wide code <pre> scrolls in its own overflow-x:auto container, not the page. Sub-pages share the identical shell/CSS.
rubric.notes: Programmatic 0px on both classes; main-page light+dark reviewed.

rubric.evidence: All cross-encoder inference runs in a module Web Worker; the page only renders the returned ranking, so the interaction path stays off the main thread and INP stays low. A single batched forward pass scores all pairs at once (309 ms for 6 pairs on WASM). 0 console errors during the run.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offload / break up long main-thread tasks so the browser can paint and respond.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the cross-encoder batch forward pass runs entirely in a module Web Worker (new Worker(WORKER_URL,{type:'module'})); the main thread only renders the ranked list.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: models/mmarco-reranker/worker.js runs the model; rerank.js renders; page run had 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy JS off the interaction path so INP stays low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference is off the main thread; clicking Rerank posts a message and awaits a worker result rather than blocking.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: RerankEngine posts {type:'run'} to the worker; UI stays responsive during the 309 ms run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid grids that wrap; avoid fixed widths; wide content scrolls in its own container; long unbreakable tokens must wrap so tables don't force page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .ba-wrap/.ct-wrap use grid repeat(auto-fit,minmax(240px,1fr)); scoped .inside-table { overflow-wrap:anywhere } wraps the long model id on mobile; the code <pre> scrolls internally.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check measured 0px overflow on desktop + mobile

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Control the clipping/overflow boundary so long content doesn't spill the layout.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the at-a-glance table cells wrap long ids/paths; the wide code sample is the only horizontally-scrolling element and it scrolls within its own <pre>.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: no clipped controls flagged at either viewport

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Accessible form controls with clear, associated labels.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the query <input> and passages <textarea> have associated <label>s; language-preset chips, top-k and Rerank are real buttons/inputs; Enter reranks.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: run-panel markup; conformance buttons-have-names derives as pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label controls; announce state in a live region; landmarks + heading order.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live #runStatus, labelled controls, keyboard-operable buttons, header/footer landmarks.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: index.html status region + sub-page status regions

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mmarco-reranker multi-model demo pairing it with BM25/lexical retrieval to show the full sparse-retrieve → multilingual-cross-encoder-rerank pipeline that mMARCO was designed for.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mmarco-reranker (cross-encoder/mmarco-mMiniLMv2-L12-H384-v1 — the MULTILINGUAL sibling of the built English MS MARCO MiniLM cross-encoder, a 12-layer XLM-RoBERTa MiniLMv2 distilled from XLM-R Large and trained on mMARCO across 14 languages, Apache-2.0). Real in-browser WASM rerank verified in headless Chrome on the actual page: a Japanese passage was promoted to #1 for an ENGLISH query across a mixed EN/JA/ES/DE corpus (309 ms, 0 console errors). Standalone cross-check confirmed a REAL relevance head (logits [5,1], not last_hidden_state) with correct cross-lingual ordering (EN-relevant 10.39, ES-relevant 10.11, unrelated recipe -8.13). fp32 dtype disclosed honestly (the repo's int8 files carry hardware-specific names transformers.js can't auto-resolve). Fresh-profile Download (~470 MB) + auto-init (no Load button) verified; responsive matrix 0px overflow on desktop + mobile, light+dark reviewed. Apache-2.0 recorded — a commercial-use advantage over the CC-BY-NC jina-reranker-v2.
